name: Luesia - Puy Moné Loop
name_original: Puy Fonguera - Cabo Bal - Puy Moné — trail running circular por la Sierra de Luesia
description: This difficult 11.4-mile trail running circular route through the Sierra de Luesia offers panoramic views and challenging terrain.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Luesia traverse a landscape characterized by significant elevation changes and natural terrain. The region features numerous hills and summits, offering challenging ascents and panoramic views. Running trails often lead through varied environments, including forested areas and open country. The network of routes caters to those seeking longer, more demanding running experiences. Best jogging routes around Luesia The most popular jogging route is Luesia - Puy Moné Loop, a 11.4 miles (18.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 29 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain, leading to expansive views. Another top favourite among local runners is Luesia Castle – Refugio de la Artica loop from Luesia, a difficult 9.7 miles (15.7 km) path. This route combines historical landmarks with natural scenery, including a mountain refuge. Local runners also love the Puy Moné Summit – Puy Fonguera Summit loop from Luesia, a 17.9 miles (28.8 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 36 minutes. Jogging around Luesia is defined by significant hills, challenging ascents, and natural trails. The network offers options primarily for experienced runners seeking demanding routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore Luesia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Luesia?
A: There are 13 dedicated running routes in the Luesia region, primarily catering to experienced runners due to their challenging nature.

Q: Are there any easy running routes in Luesia for beginners?
A: The running routes around Luesia are generally challenging, characterized by significant elevation changes and natural terrain. There are no easy routes listed, with most being difficult and a few moderate.

Q: What are the typical distances for running routes around Luesia?
A: The routes vary in length, with options ranging from approximately 15.7 km (9.7 miles) to 28.8 km (17.9 miles). For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24550583" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luesia Castle – Refugio de la Artica loop&lt;/a&gt; is 15.7 km, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38161662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puy Moné Summit – Puy Fonguera Summit loop&lt;/a&gt; extends to 28.8 km.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Luesia's running trails?
A: Luesia's running trails feature significant elevation changes, numerous hills, and summits. You'll traverse varied environments, including forested areas and open country, offering challenging ascents and panoramic views.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the Luesia area?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Luesia are circular. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983181671/luesia-puy-mone-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luesia - Puy Moné Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24550583" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luesia Castle – Refugio de la Artica loop&lt;/a&gt;, both offering a complete circuit back to the starting point.

Q: What can I see along the running routes in Luesia?
A: The routes often lead past interesting natural and historical landmarks. You might encounter natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2716013" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pígalo Pools&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5816555" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gypsum ravine&lt;/a&gt;. Historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5271588" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luesia Castle&lt;/a&gt; are also accessible on some trails.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots on the running trails?
A: Absolutely. The region's numerous hills and summits provide excellent panoramic views. Notable viewpoints include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5284678" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puy Fonguera Summit and Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5286112" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de las Ripas Altas&lt;/a&gt;, offering expansive vistas of the surrounding landscape.

Q: Can I combine running with exploring historical sites in Luesia?
A: Yes, several routes integrate historical landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24550583" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luesia Castle – Refugio de la Artica loop&lt;/a&gt; allows you to combine your run with a visit to the Luesia Castle. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38162561" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Biel Castle – View from Puy Moné loop&lt;/a&gt; which passes by Biel Castle.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Luesia?
A: Runners frequently praise the challenging ascents and the rewarding panoramic views from the summits. The natural, varied terrain, including forested areas and open country, also contributes to a highly engaging running experience.

Q: Are there any routes that lead to mountain refuges?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24550583" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Luesia Castle – Refugio de la Artica loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically includes a mountain refuge, providing a point of interest along the challenging 15.7 km path.

Q: What is the elevation gain like on Luesia's running routes?
A: The routes in Luesia are known for significant elevation changes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38161662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puy Moné Summit – Puy Fonguera Summit loop&lt;/a&gt; features an elevation gain of over 1500 meters, making for a demanding workout.
tips: The route from Luesia to Puy Fonguera is known for its varied vegetation and stunning landscapes. Furthermore, the area is rich in flora and fauna, making it an ideal destination for nature lovers.
We are at the top of Puy Moné, a beautiful place at the top of the Sierra de Luesia and from where we can see the Sierra de Santo Domingo and the pre-Pyrenees or the Moncayo.

This refuge is known for its natural surroundings and strategic location for hiking and nature lovers. It offers spectacular views and is an ideal starting point for exploring the mountains and surrounding areas.

The refuge is equipped with basic amenities for hikers, including sleeping and cooking areas. It is a perfect place to rest and enjoy the tranquility of the natural surroundings.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this trail running route?
faqs.answer: This challenging trail running route, covering approximately 18.3 km with 624 meters of elevation gain, is estimated to take around 2 hours and 30 minutes for experienced trail runners. However, given its 'difficult' rating and varied terrain, individual times may vary based on fitness and pace.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginner trail runners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is considered difficult and is best suited for experienced trail runners with very good fitness. It's consistently described as a 'hard run' due to its significant elevation gain, diverse terrain, and potentially slippery sections.

faqs.question: Where is the starting point and parking for this route?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular trail through the Sierra de Luesia. While a specific starting point isn't detailed, the presence of the Refugio de la Artica nearby suggests it could serve as a convenient landmark or potential starting point with parking options in the vicinity of Luesia.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse terrain, starting in less wooded areas before entering a 'surprising beech forest' known as the hayedo de Bal. You'll navigate through beautiful beech, oak, pine, and holm oak forests, with rich undergrowth of boxwood and holly. At higher altitudes, the vegetation gives way to extensive pastures. Be prepared for steep, zig-zagging paths and sections that can be 'somewhat broken and steep'. The descent can be particularly slippery, especially in wet conditions.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the way?
faqs.answer: The route is renowned for its great scenic richness. You'll traverse the Protected Landscape of the Sierra de Santo Domingo. From the summits of Puy Fonguera and Cabo Bal, you'll get wide views of the Sierra de Santo Domingo and the western Huesca Pyrenees. Puy Moné also offers a great panoramic view. The journey through impressive beech forests like the 'Fayar de Gozopaño' is also a highlight.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to run this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail's diverse forests and high-altitude pastures suggest that spring and autumn would offer the most pleasant conditions, with vibrant foliage and cooler temperatures. Summer might be hot, and winter could bring snow or icy conditions, making the already challenging terrain even more difficult, especially on slippery descents.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact trail are not provided, as it's located within the Protected Landscape of the Sierra de Santo Domingo, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and respect other trail users. Always check local signage for specific rules.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to access the Sierra de Luesia?
faqs.answer: The trail runs through the Protected Landscape of the Sierra de Santo Domingo. Typically, protected landscapes in Spain do not require entrance fees for general access, but specific activities or larger groups might need permits. For trail running, it's generally free, but always check official local sources for any updated regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: What essential gear should I bring for this trail running route?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and varied terrain, essential gear includes appropriate trail running shoes with good grip, especially for potentially slippery descents. Hydration (water), nutrition, a map or GPS device (like komoot's app), and weather-appropriate layers are also highly recommended. A first-aid kit is always a good idea for challenging routes.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, a small section of this route (approximately 1%) follows the Camino de Formayor. This indicates that the trail utilizes some established paths within the area.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation nearby?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near Luesia. While specific facilities directly on the trail are limited to the Refugio de la Artica, the nearby town of Luesia would be the best place to find cafes, pubs, and accommodation options.
